questions.question: Show that for any positive integer $n$, the expression $n^3 - n$ is always divisible by $6$.
questions.answer: Since $n^3 - n = (n-1)n(n+1)$ is the product of three consecutive integers, it is divisible by $3! = 6$.

steps: Further factorize it into the product of three consecutive integers: $(n-1)n(n+1)$.
Recall that in any set of $k$ consecutive integers, there must be at least one multiple of $k$.
Identify that among any three consecutive integers, at least one is divisible by $2$ and exactly one is divisible by $3$.
Conclude that the product must be divisible by $2 \times 3 = 6$.
solution.derivation: $n^3 - n = n(n^2 - 1) \\ = (n-1)n(n+1) \\ \text{The expression is the product of three consecutive integers.} \\ \text{In any three consecutive integers, there is at least one even number and exactly one multiple of } 3. \\ \text{Since the product is divisible by both } 2 \text{ and } 3, \text{ it is divisible by } \text{lcm}(2, 3) = 6. \\ \therefore n^3 - n \text{ is divisible by } 6 \text{ for all } n \in \mathbb{N}.

questions.question: A merchant has two types of oil: $560$ litres of mustard oil and $720$ litres of sunflower oil. He wants to pack these oils into tin containers of equal size such that each container is completely filled. Find the maximum capacity of such a container and the total number of containers required for both types of oil.
questions.answer: Maximum capacity is $80$ litres, and the total number of containers is $16$.

steps: Identify that the maximum capacity of the container represents the HCF of $560$ and $720$.
Use the prime factorization method or Euclid's Division Algorithm to find the HCF.
Determine the HCF by taking the product of the lowest powers of common prime factors: $2^4 \times 5 = 80$.
Calculate the number of containers for mustard oil: $560 \div 80 = 7$.
Calculate the number of containers for sunflower oil: $720 \div 80 = 9$.
solution.derivation: $560 = 2^4 \times 5^1 \times 7^1 \\ 720 = 2^4 \times 3^2 \times 5^1 \\ \text{HCF}(560, 720) = 2^4 \times 5 = 16 \times 5 = 80 \\ \text{Containers for mustard oil} = 560 \div 80 = 7 \\ \text{Containers for sunflower oil} = 720 \div 80 = 9 \\ \text{Total containers} = 7 + 9 = 16$
